findings.criterion: The stale-framing sweep leaves zero default-OFF claims about drift-refresh in src/, tests/, docs/ (dated ADR notes excepted).
findings.detail:
Two remaining default-OFF claims about drift-refresh were found in docs/ after the sweep:
1. docs/config.md line 100 still shows `verify.progressive_drift_refresh = false` with a generic comment ('progressively refresh drifted findings during plan review') — no default-ON indication. The remediation_mode entry two rows below correctly shows `= true` to convey its on-default; progressive_drift_refresh was not updated to match.
2. docs/plan-review-gate.md line 368 still reads 'each along an independent axis and inert by default' — a blanket statement covering all three floors, including drift-refresh, which is now ON. The config table at lines 372–382 of that file also does not include a progressive_drift_refresh row at all, leaving readers with no documentation of its default-on status in that reference location.
findings.remediation: In docs/config.md line 100, change `= false` to `= true` and update the comment to convey default-ON / explicit-false-is-back-out framing (mirroring the remediation_mode entry). In docs/plan-review-gate.md, update line 368's 'inert by default' to exclude drift-refresh, and add a `verify.progressive_drift_refresh | true | …` row to the configuration table documenting default-ON and the back-out.

citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart (timestamp 1783906290844586001): 'the precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic are captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging and roll up into the epic's deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d)'

Ticket 'What' section: 'measure the token/latency saving of a drift-refresh probe vs a full re-review on a representative ticket (an f1fa-shaped fixture: signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced) and record the numbers on this ticket'

findings.criterion: The token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on a representative ticket) is recorded in a comment on this ticket.
findings.detail: A comment IS present that provides a structural criteria-count analysis (~83% reduction, 2 vs ~12 agent-tier criteria). However, the criterion explicitly asks for the measurement 'on a representative ticket (an f1fa-shaped fixture: signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced)' — i.e., actual per-run wall-clock/token figures on a concrete run. The comment explicitly defers the 'precise per-run wall-clock/token figures on live traffic' to the epic's 'deferred post-deploy measurement (72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d)', stating those roll up later. The ticket body's 'What' section also says to 'record the numbers on this ticket' — the structural ratio is reasoning about the saving, not the per-run measurement the criterion describes. The original opt-in condition was 'a token/latency measurement that was never taken'; a theoretical count-based ratio does not constitute having taken that measurement.
findings.remediation: Run the drift-refresh probe and a full re-review on a representative f1fa-shaped fixture (signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced) and record the actual wall-clock duration and token counts (probe path vs full-review path) as a concrete comment on this ticket before closing.

citations.description: Ticket status comment (timestamp 1783907653288963001): 'NOT yet pushed to Gerrit and NOT closed. REMAINING: push the stack to gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, get LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1'

findings.criterion: make lint passes on the change.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged and therefore codebase-verifiable by the verification rules. There is no artifact in the repository (lint output file, CI result, badge) from which the lint pass can be confirmed. The ticket's status comment attests 'Locally green: pre-commit make lint + make typecheck passed,' but that is an unverifiable local-run attestation for a criterion that lacks the [operator-attested] tag. Since the change has not been pushed to Gerrit and has no Verified +1, no external CI result is available either.
findings.remediation: Push the change stack to Gerrit and obtain a Verified +1 (which confirms CI including make lint), then record the Gerrit change URL and the Verified +1 result as a ticket comment before closing.

citations.description: Ticket status comment (timestamp 1783907653288963001): 'NOT yet pushed to Gerrit and NOT closed'

findings.criterion: make typecheck passes on the change.
findings.detail: Same as the lint criterion: untagged (codebase-verifiable by rule), no artifact in the repository confirms a typecheck pass. The local attestation in the status comment is not sufficient evidence for an untagged criterion, and the change has not yet been submitted to Gerrit for CI verification.
findings.remediation: Obtain a Gerrit Verified +1 (which confirms CI including make typecheck) and record the Gerrit change URL as a ticket comment before closing.

citations.description: Ticket status comment (timestamp 1783907653288963001): 'NOT yet pushed to Gerrit and NOT closed. REMAINING: push the stack to gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, get LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1'

findings.criterion: make test passes on the change.
findings.detail: Same as the lint/typecheck criteria: untagged (codebase-verifiable by rule), no test-result artifact is available in the repository. The change is locally committed but not yet pushed to Gerrit; no CI Verified +1 has been granted. Test execution cannot be confirmed from reading the codebase.
findings.remediation: Obtain a Gerrit Verified +1 (which confirms CI including make test) and record the Gerrit change URL as a ticket comment before closing.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
